# TECHNICAL MANUAL EXTRACTION - PAGES 112-119
## TROUBLESHOOTING SECTION 8.5: WARNING MESSAGES (W-XXX)

### DOCUMENT HEADER INFORMATION
- **Manual**: Legacy User Manual © Eversys S.A.
- **Publication Date**: 29.06.2022
- **Page Range**: 112-161 (extracted pages 112-119)
- **Section**: 8.5. Warning messages (W-XXX) (User and Super User access)

### REFERENCE INFORMATION
- **Error Message Codifications Reference**: [101]
- **General Service Contact**: Contact your local service organisation if actions listed fail to rectify issues or other problems occur

---

## WARNING MESSAGES TABLE (W-002 through W-060)

### W-002 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Cameo: "Coffee boiler is heating..."
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler left is heating..."
- **Remedy**: If this message remains, contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-003 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler right is heating..."
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-004 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: "Water flow left too low"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-005 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: "Water flow right too low"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-006 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "Check cleaning ball dispenser"
- **Troubleshooting Questions**:
  - Blocked or broken cleaning balls?
  - Light barrier dirty?
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-007 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "The coffee machine has been cleaned without cleaning balls"
- **Troubleshooting Questions**:
  - Cleaning ball dispenser empty?
  - Blocked or broken cleaning balls?
- **Technical Details**: This warning is set if no cleaning ball has been detected
- **User Actions**:
  - Check if the cleaning ball dispenser is empty. If yes, refill it
  - Check if a cleaning ball is blocking the dispenser
- **Final Action**: If this message remains, contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-008 - Display / Touch Screen
- **Message**: "Press Continue to start the cleaning now. If you want to start the cleaning later, push the Cancel button in the upper right corner"
- **Alternative Action**: To start cleaning later, press and hold the Rinse key for five seconds to start cleaning
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-009 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "Cleaning ball dispenser missing"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-010 - CPU
- **Message**: "Invalid coffee or milk recipe"
- **Action**: No action necessary
- **Technical Details**: 
  - This message disappears with the next successful coffee product
  - Normally this message should never appear
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-011 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Brewing unit initialisation failed left"
- **Technical Issue**: The left brewing unit is blocked
- **User Check**: Please check the left brewing unit
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-012 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Brewing unit initialisation failed right"
- **Technical Issue**: The right brewing unit is blocked
- **User Check**: Please check the right brewing unit
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-013 - Machine
- **Message**: "Service necessary. Please contact your service technician"
- **Technical Trigger**: The machine has reached the predefined number of products or the service date interval has been elapsed
- **Action**: Please contact your service technician
- **Icons**: - (not visible), ✓ (action required), ✓ (reset available)

### W-014 - Water Supply
- **Message**: "Please change the water filter"
- **Technical Trigger**: Predefined water amount has been reached
- **Technical Details**: The water filter has to be changed after its life time cycle. Otherwise the boilers will be damaged due to the limescale
- **Action**: Please contact your service technician
- **Icons**: - (not visible), ✓ (action required), ✓ (reset available)

### W-017 - Grinder Module
- **Message**: 
  - "No grinder turns detected (grinder left"
  - Cameo: "Bean hopper left"
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Bean hopper rear"
- **Automatic Action**: The grinder automatically tries to start again
- **Final Action**: If this message remains, contact service organisation
- **Icons**: - (not visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-018 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk tank empty. Please refill the milk tank"
- **Action**: Refill the milk tank [55]
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-019 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Steam pressure too low for milk products"
- **Action**: Wait until the steam pressure is high enough to produce a milk product
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-020 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Top piston failure left"
- **Automatic Action**: The machine tries to unblock the piston automatically
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-021 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Top piston failure right"
- **Automatic Action**: The machine tries to unblock the piston automatically
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-022 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Bottom piston failure left"
- **Automatic Action**: The machine tries to unblock the piston automatically
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-023 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Bottom piston failure right"
- **Automatic Action**: The machine tries to unblock the piston automatically
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-024 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk tank empty. Please refill the milk tank"
- **Action**: Refill the milk tank [55]
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-025 - Grinder Module
- **Message**: "No grinder turns detected (grinder right, bean hopper front)"
- **Automatic Actions**: 
  - The grinder automatically tries to start again
  - The grinder automatically tries to unblock itself (normally that already solves the problem)
- **Escalation**: But if it couldn't unblock itself after three times, the grinder is blocked (the warning W-068 [121] is set)
- **Final Action**: Please contact your service technician
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-027 - Display / Touch Screen
- **Message**: "Date and time incorrect. Please adjust the date and time in the display menu and restart the machine"
- **Primary Action**: Turn the coffee machine off (main switch and not standby button!), wait 10 s and turn it on again (mostly that already fixes the problem)
- **If Problem Persists**: Login with super user access and:
  - Adjust the date and time at the left display (Menu > Display > Date and Time > Set Date & Time)
  - Check also if the time zone is correct on the display(s)
- **Final Action**: If the message still appear after manually setting the date and time and restarting the machine, please contact your service technician
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-028 - Display / Touch Screen
- **Message**: "Date and time incorrect. Please adjust the date and time in the display menu and restart the machine"
- **Primary Action**: Turn the coffee machine off (main switch and not standby button!), wait 10 s and turn it on again (mostly that already fixes the problem)
- **If Problem Persists**: Login with super user access and:
  - Adjust the date and time at the left display (Menu > Display > Date and Time > Set Date & Time)
  - Check also if the time zone is correct on the display(s)
- **Final Action**: If the message still appear after manually setting the date and time and restarting the machine, please contact your service technician
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-029 - CPU
- **Message**: "Failure of external EEPROM (CPU)"
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-030 - CPU
- **Message**: "Failure during backup to external EEPROM (CPU)"
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-034 - Bean Hopper
- **Message**: "No coffee powder detected (left). Please check the powder chute and press Continue"
- **User Actions**: 
  - Check the powder chute
  - Have you inserted enough powder?
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-035 - Bean Hopper
- **Message**: "No coffee powder detected (right). Please check the powder chute and press Continue"
- **User Actions**: 
  - Check the powder chute
  - Have you inserted enough powder?
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-036 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Push brew chamber back and press Continue"
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-037 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "The milk system was cleaned without detergent. Please use EvercleanTM!"
- **Technical Issue**: None or not sufficient detergent detected for a proper cleaning of the milk system
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), ✓ (action required), - (no reset)

### W-038 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Cameo: "Coffee boiler temperature sensor failure"
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler temperature sensor failure left"
- **User Check**: Please check the temperature sensor and cable
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-039 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler temperature sensor failure right"
- **User Check**: Please check the temperature sensor and cable
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-040 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk heater temperature sensor failure left"
- **User Check**: Please check the temperature sensor and cable
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-041 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk heater temperature sensor failure right"
- **User Check**: Please check the temperature sensor and cable

- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-045 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk temperature too high (left)"
- **User Check**: Please check the milk system and tubes
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: - (not visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-046 - Milk Module
- **Message**: "Milk temperature too high (right)"
- **User Check**: Please check the milk system and tubes
- **Action**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: - (not visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-048 - Display / Touch Screen
- **Message**: "During the weekend all milk products are locked!"
- **Technical Condition**: Occurs only if you have turned on Cleaning Weekend Mode (Clean/Standby menu)
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-049 - Milk Module

  - Check if the fan is dirty or blocked by beans

---

## VISUAL ELEMENTS AND INTERFACE ICONS

### Status Icons System (3-column format):
1. **Eye Icon (👁)**: Message visibility indicator
   - ✓ = Message visible to user
   - - = Message not visible to user

2. **Circular Arrow Icon (↻)**: Action required indicator
   - ✓ = Action required from user
   - - = No action required

3. **Exit/Reset Icon (🚪)**: Reset availability indicator
   - ✓ = Reset function available
   - - = No reset function available

### Color Coding System:
- **Yellow bars**: Warning level indicators (various intensities shown for different warning severities)
- **Orange bars**: Higher severity warnings
- **Red bars**: Critical warnings requiring service contact

---

## TECHNICAL PROCEDURES AND TIMING SPECIFICATIONS

### Restart Procedure (W-027, W-028):
- **Power Off Duration**: Wait 10 seconds
- **Switch Type**: Main switch (not standby button)
- **Alternative Key Sequence**: Press and hold Rinse key for 5 seconds (W-008)

### Automatic Recovery Attempts:
- **Grinder Unblock Attempts**: 3 times maximum before escalation to W-068
- **Piston Unblock**: Automatic attempts by machine system

### Service Intervals:
- **Product Count**: Predefined number of products triggers W-013
- **Time Interval**: Service date interval triggers W-013  
- **Water Filter**: Predefined water amount triggers W-014

## 7.1. Cleaning and maintenance operations

### 7.1.1. Cleaning

**Mandatory Requirements:**
- Regular cleaning is mandatory in order to ensure problem-free operation of the machine and to ensure optimum coffee quality
- Refer to method and frequency under Care [83]

**Health and Safety Requirements:**
- It is imperative to ensure that persons are not subject to any health hazards as a consequence of the consumption of the food items dispensed by the machine
- If care and cleaning of the machine is not carried out properly, dispensing milk beverages will constitute a food hygiene hazard point

**Logging and Documentation:**
- All automatic cleaning operations are logged

**Reference Information:**
- For more information on the cleaning intervals, see Periodic cleaning [85]

### 7.1.2. Maintenance

**Service Authorization Requirements:**
- Repair, commissioning and service operations must only be performed by a service organisation appointed by the manufacturer

**Visual Element Description:**
- Warning triangle symbol (⚠️) with exclamation mark
- Gray background box containing the service authorization warning text

**Service Schedule Specifications:**
- The service of Eversys coffee machines depends on the number of brewing chambers:
  - **Légacy range**: 12 months or every 50'000 products

**Contact Information:**
- Please contact your local service organisation for any maintenance tasks

## Reference Numbers Mentioned
- [83] - Care method and frequency reference
- [85] - Periodic cleaning intervals reference

# Technical Manual Extract: Pages 87-94 - Cleaning Procedures (Standard)

## 7.3 Cleaning procedures (standard)

### 7.3.1 Refill and visual check of the Eversys cleaning ball™ dispensers

#### SAFETY WARNINGS - IRRITANT PRODUCTS

**IRRITANT PRODUCTS**
- EvercleanTM and Eversys cleaning ballTM should be considered potentially dangerous
- When performing any operation on the machine, local safety regulations must be followed
- It is imperative to wear protective gloves and glasses when working with potentially dangerous materials

**MECHANICAL SAFETY**
- It is imperative to switch off the machine before carrying any operation on the cleaning balls dispenser
- There is a risk of minor injury with moving parts

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch
2. Unlock and remove the cleaning balls lid (1)
3. Remove both Eversys cleaning ballTM containers (2) and clean with a clean cloth each dispenser (3)
4. If necessary, replace new cleaning balls containers. Remove the dispensing system (4) from the old container and replace it on the new one
5. Replace and lock the cleaning balls lid (1)
6. Switch on [49] at the main power switch

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Cleaning balls lid
- Item 2: Eversys cleaning ballTM containers
- Item 3: Dispensers
- Item 4: Dispensing system

### 7.3.2 Automatic system cleaning procedure with Eversys milk cleaning ballTM on Légacy machines (milk and powder unit included)

**General Information:**
- It is possible to run the complete cleaning procedure at any time
- Though you must run the cleaning when requested (if cancelled and depending on settings, it will not be possible to make a drink unless cleaning is performed)

#### Procedure Steps:

**Step 1: Initiate Cleaning**
1. Press and hold the rinse (1) button for 3 s to run automatic cleaning

**Step 2: Confirmation**
2. Press Yes (or No to cancel)

**Step 3: Grounds Drawer**
3. Empty and clean the grounds drawer [62]
4. Press Continue

**Step 5: Milk System (if detected - machine model)**
- Remove the milk tank (2) and store it in a fridge during the cleaning process
- If necessary clean the milk tank with warm water and soft soap. Rinse thoroughly
- Place the milk tube inside the cleaning tank (3). Ensure that it correctly lays at the bottom of the tank
- Place the empty cleaning tank inside of the fridge
- Press Continue

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Rinse button
- Item 2: Milk tank
- Item 3: Cleaning tank

**Step 6: Choco module / powder unit (machine option)**

**Panel Removal:**
- Unlock and push at the bottom of the panel (4) to remove it

**Tube Disconnection:**
- Unplug the tube (5) from the panel

**Mixer Removal:**
- Turn the handle upwards (CCW) (6) to unlock the mixer (7)
- Gently remove the mixer (7)

**Component References:**
- Item 4: Panel
- Item 5: Tube
- Item 6: Handle (CCW = Counter-clockwise)
- Item 7: Mixer

**Mixer Disassembly:**
- Separate the mixer (tube (5), powder chanel (8), base (9) and cover (10))
- Clean all under clean tap water with soft soap. Dry the parts

**Component References:**
- Item 5: Tube
- Item 8: Powder channel
- Item 9: Base
- Item 10: Cover

**Mixer Reassembly:**
- Assemble the mixer (tube (5), cover (8), powder chanel (9) and base (10)). Replace it inside the choco module/powder unit
- Lock the handle (6) and plug the tube (5)

**Component References:**
- Item 5: Tube
- Item 6: Handle
- Item 8: Cover
- Item 9: Powder channel
- Item 10: Base

**Panel Replacement:**
- Replace and lock the panel (4)
- Press Continue

**Component References:**
- Item 4: Panel

**Step 7: Cleaning Process**
7. Wait for the cleaning to complete

**RISK OF BURNS OR SCALDING**
- During automatic cleaning, hot cleaning solution and steam are released several times
- Keep hands and skin away from the outlets

**Timing Information:**
- It takes approximately 12 minutes to complete the entire process

**Step 8: Post-Cleaning Milk System (if detected - machine model)**
- When cleaning is finished, remove and clean the cleaning tank (3)
- Empty the cleaning tank in a sink
- Clean the milk tank tube with a damp single-use cloth and replace the milk tank (2) in the fridge
- If necessary, fill the milk tank [55]
- Ensure that milk tank tube correctly lays at the bottom of the tank

**Temperature Specification:**
- max. 3°C (37°F)

**Component References:**
- Item 2: Milk tank
- Item 3: Cleaning tank

**Step 9: Grounds Drawer Final Steps**
9. Empty the grounds drawer in a sink and clean it with soft soap

**RISK OF BURNS OR SCALDING**
- After first startup or during cleaning, the grounds drawer may contain hot water, empty it carefully
- Reference: Automatic system cleaning procedure with Eversys milk cleaning ballTM on Légacy machines (milk and powder unit included) [87]

**Final Steps:**
10. Rinse and dry the grounds drawer
11. Replace the grounds drawer inside the machine
12. Press Yes to acknowledge emptying of the grounds drawer (or No to cancel)
13. Your machine is now clean and ready for use

### 7.3.3 Clean the coffee outlet (milk outlet and single outlet included)

**Special Note:**
- If you have the optional choco module or powder unit ensure to Clean the choco outlet (machine option) [96]

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch
2. Gently pull down (1) to remove the coffee outlet
3. Clean with soft soap under clean water (2)
4. Gently reassemble in the reverse order

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Coffee outlet removal direction
- Item 2: Cleaning process
- Outlet types: Single, Double

### 7.3.4 Empty and clean the bean hoppers (and powder chute (machine option))

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch
2. Remove the bean hopper lids (1)
3. Unlock the bean hoppers (2) (Closed position)
4. Remove the bean hoppers (3) and store the beans for re-use
5. Clean the bean hoppers (4) with soft soap, rinse with water and dry with a soft cloth
6. Reassemble in the reverse order and lock the bean hoppers (5) (Open position)
7. Fill the coffee bean hoppers [54]

**Important Note:**
- Make sure that the bean hoppers are perfectly dry before refilling with coffee beans

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Bean hopper lids
- Item 2: Bean hoppers (Closed position)
- Item 3: Bean hoppers (removal)
- Item 4: Bean hoppers (cleaning)
- Item 5: Bean hoppers (Open position)

**Position Settings:**
- Close position: For unlocking
- Open position: For locking

### 7.3.5 Clean the housing

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch
2. Clean the housing with a damp cloth

### 7.3.6 Clean the display (touch screen)

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Press on the top middle of the touch screen (1)
2. Press on the Clean the screen button (2)
3. During the countdown, disinfect the screen according to the following recommendation

**Cleaning Instructions:**
- Only use commercial disinfectants for hands. Do not use any sanitary disinfectants!
- Spray disinfectant on a soft cloth and carefully wipe the surface with the cloth
- **Important! Never spray any liquid directly onto the surface**

**System Feature:**
- The countdown allows to clean the screen without the risk of starting a product

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Touch screen top middle
- Item 2: Clean the screen button

**Display Information:**
- Countdown timer shows: "10 s"
- Screen displays: "Clean the screen now"

### 7.3.7 Clean the drip tray

#### Procedure Steps:
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch
2. Remove the drip tray grid (1)
3. Wash the grid and inside of the drip tray (2) with soft soap and rinse
4. Replace the drip tray grid (1)

**Component References:**
- Item 1: Drip tray grid
- Item 2: Drip tray interior

## 7.4. CLEANING PROCEDURES (DEPENDING ON THE MODEL AND OPTIONS OF THE MACHINE)

### 7.4.1. Empty and clean the milk tank and the inside of the fridge (machine option)

**SAFETY WARNING:**
- Always clean your hands before refilling milk. Germs can be introduced from dirty hands when opening.

**MILK SPECIFICATIONS:**
- Only use cooled milk (pasteurised, homogenised or UHT)

**AVAILABILITY NOTE:**
- Depending on machine model and options, this feature may not be available. For more information, contact your service organisation.

**EQUIPMENT VARIATION:**
- There are many different refrigerators on the market, so please note that your fridge may look different to the configurations shown below.

**PROCEDURE STEPS:**
1. Open the door.
2. Remove the milk tank.
3. Remove the milk tank lid.
4. Clean all parts (milk tank tube(s) included) and the inside of the fridge with soft soap, rinse with water and dry with a soft cloth.
5. Reassemble in the reverse order.
6. If necessary, fill the milk tank [55].
7. Close the door.

**VISUAL CONTENT:**
- Diagram showing coffee machine with milk tank removal process
- Inset detail showing proper handling technique for milk tank components

### 7.4.2. Clean the choco outlet (machine option)

**AVAILABILITY NOTE:**
- Depending on machine model and options, this feature may not be available. For more information, contact your service organisation.

**PROCEDURE STEPS:**
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch.
2. Gently pull down (1) to remove the choco outlet.
3. Clean with soft soap under clean water (2).
4. Gently reassemble in the reverse order.

**VISUAL CONTENT:**
- Two-step diagram labeled "1" and "2" showing:
  - Step 1: Downward arrow indicating pull-down motion to remove choco outlet
  - Step 2: Cleaning under running water with soap icons (faucet and soap dispenser symbols)

### 7.4.3. Empty and clean the powder container(s) (machine option)

**AVAILABILITY NOTE:**
- Depending on machine model and options, this feature may not be available. For more information, contact your service organisation.

**PROCEDURE STEPS:**
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch.
2. Remove the powder container lid (1).
3. Pull the container and lift to remove it (2).
4. Clean all parts with soft soap, rinse with water and dry with a soft cloth.
5. Reassemble in the reverse order.

**CRITICAL WARNING:**
- Make sure that the powder containers and powder chutes are perfectly dry before refilling with powder.

**VISUAL CONTENT:**
- Two-step diagram showing:
  - Step 1: Removal of powder container lid with upward arrow
  - Step 2: Container removal process with directional arrows and "no dishwasher" symbol

### 7.4.4. Clean the steam wand

**FREQUENCY REQUIREMENT:**
- After every use of the steam wand (steam arm) it is necessary to wipe and purge it.

**PROCEDURE STEPS:**
1. Press the blinking purge button on the main screen (1).
2. Wipe the steam wand with a damp cloth (2). Change cloths every day.
3. On the screen that displays, press purge (3).

**VISUAL CONTENT:**
- Three-step process showing:
  - Interface screenshot with purge button highlighted (labeled "1")
  - Steam wand being wiped with cloth (labeled "2")
  - Second interface screen with purge confirmation (labeled "3")
  - Steam wand cleaning icons and hand gesture indicators

### 7.4.5. Clean the steam nozzle

**AVAILABILITY NOTE:**
- Depending on machine model and options, this feature may not be available. For more information, contact your service organisation.

**FREQUENCY REQUIREMENT:**
- Only necessary to clean the nozzle monthly or if required.

**PROCEDURE STEPS:**
1. Switch off [63] at the main power switch.
2. Remove the nozzle (1).
3. Wash the nozzle with soft soap and clean water using a soft brush (2).
4. Tighten the nozzle (3).

**VISUAL CONTENT:**
- Three-step diagram showing:
  - Step 1: Nozzle removal with rotational arrow
  - Step 2: Cleaning process with brush, soap, and water symbols
  - Step 3: Reinstallation with tightening rotation arrow

## Section 6.4: Product & Keys Menu (Manager Access)

### Technical Specifications

#### Hot Water Quantity Settings:
- **Steam Boiler Dispensing**: Set in seconds
- **Coffee Boiler Dispensing**: Set in ticks (controlled by flowmeter)
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 1000 ticks
- **Conversion**: Ticks are approximately equal to ml

#### Milk Quantity Settings:
- **Unit**: Set in seconds
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 100 seconds

### User Interface Elements and Controls

The interface shows numbered control elements (1-10) with the following functions:

#### Primary Controls (1-7):
1. **Key Name**: Press to type in a new name (maximum 13 characters)
2. **Key Icon**: Press to select another product picture
3. **Water Quantity**: Press to modify the number of ticks or seconds (adjust with the slider or up/down keys)
4. **Learn Quantity**:
   - a. Press Learn Quantity to dispense a coffee
   - b. When the desired level is in the cup, press the same button to stop the drink. This memorises the number of ticks (or seconds)
5. **Browse to the next menu**
6. **Milk Sequence**: Press one of the line under the Milk sequence to open available settings (only for milk products and if you have a fridge)
7. **Powder Sequence**: Press the line under the Powder sequence to open available settings (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit)

#### Secondary Controls (8-10):
8. **Milk Quantity**: (only for milk products and if you have a fridge), press to modify the numbers of seconds or Learn Quantity
9. **Water Quantity**: (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit), press to modify the quantity (in ml)
10. **Product Test**

### Product Test Requirements:
- **Minimum Test Runs**: Ensure that at least 6 beverages are run using Product Test
- **Purpose**: To achieve the desired coffee and extraction time
- **Verification**: That the grinder is delivering the correct amount of coffee for the set cake thickness
- **Adjustment Reference**: To adjust see Bean & Grinder menu [78]

## WARNING MESSAGES TABLE (W-002 through W-060)

### W-002 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Cameo: "Coffee boiler is heating..."
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler left is heating..."
- **Remedy**: If this message remains, contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-003 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: 
  - Enigma / Shotmaster / E'line: "Coffee boiler right is heating..."
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-004 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: "Water flow left too low"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-005 - Hydraulic Module
- **Message**: "Water flow right too low"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-006 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "Check cleaning ball dispenser"
- **Troubleshooting Questions**:
  - Blocked or broken cleaning balls?
  - Light barrier dirty?
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-007 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "The coffee machine has been cleaned without cleaning balls"
- **Troubleshooting Questions**:
  - Cleaning ball dispenser empty?
  - Blocked or broken cleaning balls?
- **Technical Details**: This warning is set if no cleaning ball has been detected
- **User Actions**:
  - Check if the cleaning ball dispenser is empty. If yes, refill it
  - Check if a cleaning ball is blocking the dispenser
- **Final Action**: If this message remains, contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-008 - Display / Touch Screen
- **Message**: "Press Continue to start the cleaning now. If you want to start the cleaning later, push the Cancel button in the upper right corner"
- **Alternative Action**: To start cleaning later, press and hold the Rinse key for five seconds to start cleaning
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-009 - Cleaning Balls Dispenser
- **Message**: "Cleaning ball dispenser missing"
- **Remedy**: Contact service organisation
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), - (no reset)

### W-010 - CPU
- **Message**: "Invalid coffee or milk recipe"
- **Action**: No action necessary
- **Technical Details**: 
  - This message disappears with the next successful coffee product
  - Normally this message should never appear
- **Icons**: ✓ (visible), - (no action), ✓ (reset available)

### W-011 - Brewing Module
- **Message**: "Brewing unit initialisation failed left"
- **Technical Issue**: The left brewing unit is blocked

## Section 6.4: Product & Keys Menu (Manager Access)

### Technical Specifications

#### Hot Water Quantity Settings:
- **Steam Boiler Dispensing**: Set in seconds
- **Coffee Boiler Dispensing**: Set in ticks (controlled by flowmeter)
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 1000 ticks
- **Conversion**: Ticks are approximately equal to ml

#### Milk Quantity Settings:
- **Unit**: Set in seconds
- **Adjustment Range**: 0 to 100 seconds

### User Interface Elements and Controls

The interface shows numbered control elements (1-10) with the following functions:

#### Primary Controls (1-7):
1. **Key Name**: Press to type in a new name (maximum 13 characters)
2. **Key Icon**: Press to select another product picture
3. **Water Quantity**: Press to modify the number of ticks or seconds (adjust with the slider or up/down keys)
4. **Learn Quantity**:
   - a. Press Learn Quantity to dispense a coffee
   - b. When the desired level is in the cup, press the same button to stop the drink. This memorises the number of ticks (or seconds)
5. **Browse to the next menu**
6. **Milk Sequence**: Press one of the line under the Milk sequence to open available settings (only for milk products and if you have a fridge)
7. **Powder Sequence**: Press the line under the Powder sequence to open available settings (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit)

#### Secondary Controls (8-10):
8. **Milk Quantity**: (only for milk products and if you have a fridge), press to modify the numbers of seconds or Learn Quantity
9. **Water Quantity**: (only for powder products and if you have a choco module or a powder unit), press to modify the quantity (in ml)
10. **Product Test**

### Product Test Requirements:
- **Minimum Test Runs**: Ensure that at least 6 beverages are run using Product Test
- **Purpose**: To achieve the desired coffee and extraction time
- **Verification**: That the grinder is delivering the correct amount of coffee for the set cake thickness
- **Adjustment Reference**: To adjust see Bean & Grinder menu [78]

### Product Type Configurations

#### Section 6.4.1: EverfoamTM and Auto Foam Products
**Controllable Parameters**:
- Stop Temperature
- Foam Texture

## SECTION 5.1: DAILY SETUP GUIDELINES

### QUALITY NOTICE (Information Box with "!" Icon)
The quality of the coffee depends on many factors, but it is most important of all to use fresh coffee beans and to regularly clean and service your machine according to Cleaning and maintenance operations [84].

### STEP-BY-STEP DAILY SETUP PROCEDURE

**1.** Switch on [49] at the main power switch.
- Or wake up [49] with the standby button.

**2.** If necessary, acknowledge errors (see Error message example [100]).

**3.** Press Rinse [51] to initialise startup.

**4.** Press Steam purge button [53] to initialise startup (machine option).

**5.** Empty and clean the grounds drawer [62].

**6.** Fill with fresh coffee bean [54].

**7.** Fill the powder containers (machine option) [56].

**8.** Fill a milk tank (machine option) [55].

**9.** Fill the cups heating surface with cups (machine option) [57].

**10.** If necessary, fill with Eversys cleaning balls™ [87].

**11.** Enjoy your drink!

## 2.5. Coffee Process Flow

### Overview Description
Depending on the process/product, the system will take suitable quantities of beans and grind them according to the product needs, supply them to the appropriate brew chamber and tamp according to settings. It will then brew and dispense the product according to your request.

### Visual Content Description
The page contains a detailed diagram showing the coffee brewing process with numbered components (1-5) against a red background. The diagram illustrates hands operating various components of the coffee machine system, including:
- Component 1: Grinding mechanism with beans falling into grinder
- Component 2: Portafilter handling and leveling
- Component 3: Temperature control system with gauges
- Component 4: Brewing process with espresso extraction
- Component 5: Milk frothing pitcher

### Technical Process Steps

#### 1. Grinding
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Burr Type**: Eversys-designed ceramic burrs
- **Motor Characteristics**: Powerful yet quiet motor
- **Heat Management**: Heat is cleverly channelled away from the coffee beans to maintain their quality via a set of fans
- **Control System**: Electronic control maintains consistency of product throughout the day, ensuring optimal extraction at all times

#### 2. e'Levelling and Tamping
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Levelling Process**: Automatic levelling initiates movement to the brewing chamber, creates vibration which enables powder in the chamber to spread more evenly
- **Brewing Chamber Capacity**: 24 gram brewing chamber
- **Tamping Control**: Electronic tamping to guarantee consistent quality
- **Function**: Facilitates single cycle large beverages
- **Barista Replication**: Movement is a replica of what the barista does - tap the portafilter
- **Result**: Leads to better, more consistent extraction

#### 3. Temperature Control
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Boiler Configuration**: Dedicated coffee boiler, separated from the water/steam boiler
- **Function**: Ensures productivity and temperature control
- **Quality Impact**: Essential parts of in-cup quality

#### 4. Brewing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Extraction Method**: Reverse gravity extraction vertical infusion
- **Efficiency**: Optimises powder efficiency as the espresso is produced

#### 5. Milk Frothing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Frothing Method**: Milk is frothed with air only in the one-step system
- **Quality Features**: Promoting flavour as well as texture
- **Experience**: Mirroring the Barista experience

#### 6. Dispensing
**Technical Specifications:**
- **Programming**: All products are pre-programmed to be dispensed in a consistent and efficient manner
- **Objectives**: Placing seamless productivity and quality at the forefront of the customer experience
